creator: michaelbuch12@gmail.com
text:
Created attachment 233107
Thread backtraces

Starting today, wifimgr consistently crashes after entering a password. The main wifimgr window appears briefly, but then immediately crashes. Below is the stderr/stdout log:

$ uname -a
FreeBSD apia 13.0-RELEASE-p4 FreeBSD 13.0-RELEASE-p4 #0: Tue Aug 24 07:33:27 UTC 2021     root@amd64-builder.daemonology.net:/usr/obj/usr/src/amd64.amd64/sys/GENERIC  amd64

$ wifimgr

(wifimgr:36938): Pango-WARNING **: 18:15:55.022: Invalid UTF-8 string passed to pango_layout_set_text()
dbus[36938]: arguments to dbus_message_iter_append_basic() were incorrect, assertion "_dbus_check_is_valid_utf8 (*string_p)" failed in file dbus-message.c line 2760.
This is normally a bug in some application using the D-Bus library.

  D-Bus not built with -rdynamic so unable to print a backtrace
Abort trap (core dumped)

#################################################################################

I have a corefile locally and attached backtraces of all threads to this bug report, though I'm not sure this is any more useful (since, from the logs, we know exactly which assert caused the abort).

I installed wifimgr earlier today using:
$ sudo pkg install wifimgr

Let me know if you need me to provide more info.
